food_subgroup: Processed poultry — breaded and fried chicken nuggets with spicy seasoning, served as a meal set

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002490 covers chicken nugget food product as closest parent; no specific code for fiery/rotisserie-style spiced nugget meal set exists in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, processed — breaded/battered poultry product
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A039V covers processed breaded poultry as closest parent. No specific code for spicy rotisserie-style chicken nugget meal set exists in FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific commercial chicken nugget meal product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a 5-piece fiery rotisserie chicken nugget meal comprising approximately 5 nuggets (roughly 175 g) plus a side dish and beverage, totalling approximately 350 g of solid food.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken nuggets, breaded, fried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05295); adapted with spicy seasoning adjustment from USDA Foundation Foods chicken nugget entries
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for breaded fried chicken nuggets (NDB 05295 / FDC ID 171477). Spicy seasoning adds negligible macro change at typical application rates (<2 g per 100 g product). Sodium is adjusted upward by approximately 80 mg/100 g to reflect commercial spicy seasoning blend typical of fast-food fiery variants. Values represent the nugget component only; meal-set side and beverage are not included in per-100g figures.

gi_source.title: Chicken nuggets, fried, breaded — GI analogous estimate based on Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283; breaded fried chicken products GI ~50
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for fiery rotisserie-style chicken nuggets. GI of 50 is estimated from the closest analogous food: breaded fried chicken products reported in Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of GI and GL values (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283), which report GI ~50 for breaded fried chicken. GL per serving calculated as (50 × 16.5 g carb per 100 g × 175 g nugget portion / 100) / 100 = approximately 14.4.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~580 mg/100 g; ~1015 mg per 175 g nugget portion)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this product due to its high sodium content; a single 175 g nugget serving provides approximately 44% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by the American Heart Association.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~50) and moderate carbohydrate load from breaded coating, combined with high fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this product in controlled portions; the breaded coating contributes moderate glycemic load (~14 GL per serving) and the high fat content may impair postprandial insulin sensitivity, so pairing with non-starchy vegetables and limiting portion to 3–4 pieces is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol from deep-frying in commercial oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this product as it contains saturated fat (~3.4 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), cholesterol (~48 mg/100 g), and high sodium, all of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk per AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fiery Rotisserie Chicken Nuggets Meal (5 pieces) is a Western-style fast-food meal set sold in Singapore, consisting of five bite-sized chicken nuggets coated in a spiced breadcrumb crust with a fiery chilli-based seasoning inspired by rotisserie flavour profiles, served alongside a side dish and beverage. The nuggets are made from processed chicken meat that is marinated in a bold spice blend featuring chilli, paprika, and garlic, then coated in seasoned breadcrumbs and deep-fried until golden and crispy on the outside while remaining juicy inside. The flavour is intensely spicy and savoury with a smoky undertone from the rotisserie-style seasoning. Nutritionally, the nuggets are energy-dense at approximately 263 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein content of 15.8 g/100 g, significant fat at 15.2 g/100 g from deep-frying, and a moderate carbohydrate contribution of 16.5 g/100 g from the breadcrumb coating. Sodium is notably high at approximately 580 mg/100 g, making this a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 50 (low-medium boundary), placing it in the low GI category, though the glycemic load per serving is moderate at ~14. Clinically, this product is classified as ultra-processed and is not recommended for frequent consumption by individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. It is commonly found on field survey data platforms under Western and Fried Chicken cuisine categories.
common_user_queries: How many calories in fiery chicken nuggets meal?
Is fiery rotisserie chicken nuggets meal healthy?
Sodium content of spicy chicken nuggets Singapore
Spicy chicken nuggets meal calories Singapore
Is chicken nuggets meal halal Singapore?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for this specific commercial product (Fiery Rotisserie Chicken 5pc Nuggets Meal from field survey data) was found in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy breaded fried chicken nugget data (FDC ID 171477) with a sodium adjustment for commercial spicy seasoning. GI is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. (2008). Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477, breaded fried chicken nuggets) with sodium adjusted upward by ~80 mg/100 g to reflect commercial fiery seasoning. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this product category. 3. Vitamin C is reported as 0.0 mg/100 g; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for breaded fried chicken nuggets — confirmed zero. 4. GI of 50 is an analogous estimate based on breaded fried chicken products in Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of GI and GL Values, Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283. 5. The meal set includes a side dish and beverage; per-100g values represent the nugget component only. 6. Halal status is assumed based on the Singapore fast-food context and field survey listing; formal halal certification should be verified with the specific restaurant. 7. Trans fat is reported as 0.1 g/100 g reflecting trace levels typical of commercial frying oils; this is not confirmed zero. 8. The food_id GD000344 is preserved from the input seed record.
